Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=400}

Run.Text: Shipboard helicopters are one of the primary methods for a surface ship or Surface Action Group (SAG) to recon an area, since the SH-2F (and the Soviet Ka-25/Ka-27) carry an airborne surface search radar.

Once the helicopter has been launched, you can select it by clicking on it in the Tactical Display, or clicking on it within the Formation Manager. Then use the sensor options to turn on its surface search radar.
Once the helicopter's radar is on, we want to get it up to altitude to extend its radar horizon. Use the Altitude button at the bottom left to order the helicopter up to 10,000' altitude.
